questions.question_text:
Two varieties of mango, A and B, are available at prices Rs. p1 and Rs. p2 per kg,
respectively. One buyer buys 5 kg. of A and 10 kg. of B and another buyer spends
Rs 100 on A and Rs. 150 on B. If the average expenditure per mango (irrespective of
variety) is the same for the two buyers, then which of the following statements is the
most appropriate?

For a given bivariate data set (xi, yi; i = 1, 2, . . . , n) , the squared correlation coeﬃcient
(r2) between x2 and y is found to be 1. Which of the following statements is the most
appropriate?
options: In the (x, y) scatter diagram, all points lie on a straight line.
In the (x, y) scatter diagram, all points lie on the curve y = x2.
In the (x, y) scatter diagram, all points lie on the curve y = a+bx2, a > 0, b > 0

In the (x, y) scatter diagram, all points lie on the curve y = a + bx2, a, b any
real numbers.
